title: Phantom Data Source
description: Schema for a single data source declaration. v0.13.0+ (R3.C). Used for BOTH bundled (bundles/spark/data-sources/<id>/data_source.yaml) AND operator-uploaded (/app/data/user_data_sources/<id>/data_source.yaml) sources.

schema_version.description: YAML schema version. Pinned at 1 in R3.C; future evolutions bump.

id.description: URL-safe slug, unique within origin. Matches the directory name on disk. Long enough to accommodate the bundled migration's <pack>__<rule>__<dataset> identifier.

pack_name.description: Existing 3-tuple identity component (matches catalog row's pack_name).

rule_name.description: Existing 3-tuple identity component (matches catalog row's rule_name).

dataset_name.description: Existing 3-tuple identity component (matches catalog row's dataset_name).

product.description: Product within the vendor (e.g. 'FortiGate', 'Defender for Endpoint').

how_to_use.description: Operator-facing instructions on how to use this data source for simulation: multi-dataset handling, CEF vs JSON wire format selection, sentinel values, MR-fire requirements, lessons learned that affect synthetic event construction. Multi-line markdown allowed.

categories.description: 0-3 short labels. Each maps to a marketplace badge (SIEM, EDR, Network, Cloud, IAM, etc.). Source values come from cortex pack_metadata.categories[].

validated.description: v0.17.91 — MAPPING-validated: the source was smoke-tested end-to-end against the live XSIAM tenant and its modeling rule populates xdm.* (or, when there is no modeling rule, its parsing rule extracts the columns). Renders the green 'Mapping Validated' pill on the Browse card. Mutually exclusive with raw_validated.

raw_validated.description: v0.17.146 — RAW-validated: the vendor's content pack is NOT installed on the tenant so parsing/modeling cannot be exercised, but a raw-dataset query (dataset = X | fields <rule columns>) confirmed the synthetic data lands the exact field names the rule WOULD read. Proven-correct shape, ready-to-map on install. Renders the amber 'Raw Validated' pill. Mutually exclusive with validated.

origin.description: Provenance marker. 'bundle' = shipped in the agent image; 'user' = operator-uploaded into /app/data/user_data_sources/.

author.description: Who created the YAML (e.g. 'phantom-bundle', operator name).

uploaded_by.description: For origin=user, the operator who uploaded. Null for origin=bundle.

created_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when this YAML was first created.

logo.description: Inline base64-encoded logo (SVG preferred). For bundled sources the logo lives at vendor_svgs/ and this can be null; for user sources this is the primary path.

data.description: Base64-encoded logo bytes. SVG-preferred. Raster fallback up to 512x512. Max 50KB pre-base64 (operator UI enforces).

license.description: License string (MIT, CC0, etc.). Required for non-internal sources.

fidelity.description: Visual quality marker. 'branded' = full-color vendor logo; 'branded-recolored' = vendor logo in alternate brand colors; 'monochrome-brand' = single-color brand mark; 'wordmark' = text-only vendor name; 'approximate' = best-effort fallback.

formats.description: Log formats this data source's records can be emitted as. Used by the worker to choose syntax.

is_rawlog_only.description: True when this pack ships only a raw-log dataset (no structured fields). Mirrors data_sources_store.is_rawlog_only column.

fields.description: Vendor field schema. Drives both the catalog display (field_count) and the schema_override the agent passes to phantom_create_data_worker for vendor-faithful generation.

use_cases.description: v0.17.34+ — operator-curated product-type labels. Source of truth: scripts/curate_vendor_use_cases.py's CURATION dict. UI surfaces these as filter chips + card badges. Empty array allowed.

Field.description: One vendor field. Drives both display (field_count) and value generation (xlog's dynamic_schema picks values by type + field-name patterns).

name.description: Field name as it appears in generated records (top-level JSON key OR equivalent in CEF/SYSLOG).

type.description: Field type from the controlled vocabulary. Unknown types fall back to xlog's name-pattern matching.

is_array.description: When true, the value is a 1-3 element list of the type.

is_meta.description: True for fields injected by Cortex at ingestion (_id, _time, _vendor) rather than emitted by the source. Meta fields are omitted from generated records.

example.description: Sample value, used for UI/docs only (not for generation).

enum_values.description: Required when type=enum. xlog picks one at random per record.

regex_pattern.description: Required when type=regex. xlog generates values matching this regex.

observable_override.description: Optional Rosetta observable name (e.g. 'srcHost', 'remotePort') for value generation. Lets the operator force a specific generator without picking from the type vocabulary.
